title: Skill CLI Commands

story.i_want_to: manage skills (list, create, update, import, archive, view stats and version history) via the loopctl CLI with support for reading SKILL.md files and multiple output formats
story.so_that: I can manage orchestrator skills from the terminal without crafting raw API calls, and import skill files directly from my local skill directories

rationale: The CLI is the primary interface for developers interacting with loopctl. Skill management commands enable the full lifecycle: create skills from local SKILL.md files, view version history, check performance stats, and archive obsolete skills. The import command bridges local file-based skill development with loopctl's versioned store. JSON-default output ensures agents can also use these commands programmatically.

acceptance_criteria.description: 'loopctl skill list' sends GET /api/v1/skills and displays all active skills. Supports --project flag to filter by project, --status flag to include archived. Default output is JSON; --format human shows a table with name, current_version, status, and description (truncated).

'loopctl skill get <name>' sends GET /api/v1/skills (filtered by name) and displays the skill with its current version prompt_text. Supports --version <N> flag to show a specific version by sending GET /api/v1/skills/:id/versions/:version.

'loopctl skill create --name <name> --file <path>' reads the file contents as prompt_text and sends POST /api/v1/skills. Supports --project <project> to scope to a project. If the file is a SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, the name is extracted from frontmatter (overridden by --name if provided), description from frontmatter, and prompt_text from the body after frontmatter.
'loopctl skill update <name> --file <path>' reads the file contents and sends POST /api/v1/skills/:id/versions to create a new version. Supports --changelog <text> to provide a changelog message.

'loopctl skill stats <name>' sends GET /api/v1/skills/:id/stats and displays performance statistics by version. --format human shows a table with version, avg_findings, false_positive_rate, stories_reviewed, avg_iterations.
'loopctl skill history <name>' sends GET /api/v1/skills/:id/versions and displays the version history. --format human shows a table with version, created_by, changelog (truncated), and inserted_at.

'loopctl skill import <directory> --project <project>' reads all SKILL.md files from the directory, parses frontmatter to extract name and description, uses file body as prompt_text, and sends POST /api/v1/skills/import. Reports summary: N created, N updated, N unchanged.
'loopctl skill archive <name>' sends DELETE /api/v1/skills/:id (which archives the skill). Prints confirmation message.

All commands handle API errors gracefully: 401 (not authenticated), 403 (insufficient role), 404 (not found), 422 (validation error) display human-readable error messages and exit with non-zero status code.

SKILL.md frontmatter parsing supports YAML frontmatter delimited by '---' lines. Expected fields: name (string), description (string). Any content after the closing '---' is the prompt_text. Files without frontmatter use the entire content as prompt_text and require --name flag.

test_cases.name: skill list returns skills in JSON format
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid server URL and user API key
Server has 2 active skills for the tenant
expected_results: Output is valid JSON
JSON contains array of 2 skill objects with name, current_version, status
Exit code is 0

test_cases.name: skill get displays current version prompt
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid server URL and user API key
expected_results: Output includes the full prompt_text of version 2
Output includes skill name, version number, description
Exit code is 0

test_cases.name: skill get with --version flag shows specific version
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid server URL and user API key
expected_results: Output includes the full prompt_text of version 1 (not current version 3)
Exit code is 0

test_cases.name: skill create from SKILL.md with frontmatter
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid server URL and user API key

File /tmp/test_skill.md exists with content: '---
name: loopctl:test
description: Test skill
---
Review the code for bugs...'
expected_results: Skill created with name='loopctl:test', description='Test skill'
prompt_text contains 'Review the code for bugs...' (body after frontmatter)
Exit code is 0

test_cases.name: skill import from directory
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid server URL and user API key
Directory /tmp/skills/ contains 3 SKILL.md files with frontmatter
expected_results: Output reports summary: N created, N updated, N unchanged
Exit code is 0

test_cases.name: skill stats displays performance by version
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid server URL and user API key
Server has skill 'loopctl:review' with 2 versions and performance data
steps: Run: loopctl skill stats loopctl:review --format human
expected_results: Output is a table with columns: version, avg_findings, false_positive_rate, stories_reviewed, avg_iterations
Both versions are listed with correct aggregate data
Exit code is 0

test_cases.name: skill command with invalid name shows error
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid server URL and user API key
expected_results: Output includes error message: 'Skill not found: nonexistent:skill'
Exit code is non-zero

test_cases.name: skill create without --name and no frontmatter shows error
preconditions: loopctl CLI configured with valid server URL and user API key
File /tmp/no_frontmatter.md exists with plain text content (no YAML frontmatter)
expected_results: Output includes error message indicating --name is required when file has no frontmatter
Exit code is non-zero

technical_notes: Add Loopctl.CLI.SkillCommand module following the pattern of existing CLI command modules (e.g., Loopctl.CLI.ProjectCommand from US-14.2).

SKILL.md frontmatter parsing: split on '---' delimiter (first occurrence after line 1 is the closing delimiter). Parse the content between delimiters as YAML (use :yamerl or a simple key-value parser for the limited frontmatter fields). Everything after the closing delimiter is prompt_text.
Directory import: File.ls!/1 to list files, filter for *.md extension, parse each file, assemble the import payload, send POST /api/v1/skills/import.

Name resolution for get/update/stats/history/archive: the CLI uses the skill name (e.g., 'loopctl:review') as the identifier. Send GET /api/v1/skills?name=<exact_name> to resolve the name to an ID, then use the ID for subsequent requests.
Output formatting: JSON is default (raw API response). --format human uses a table formatter (same pattern as other CLI commands). Consider extracting a shared table formatter if not already present.
Error handling: wrap all HTTP calls in a result tuple pattern. Map HTTP status codes to user-friendly messages. Exit with System.halt(1) on errors.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.
Config-based DI: behaviours resolved via Application.get_env(:loopctl, :key, DefaultModule). config/test.exs maps behaviours to Mox mocks. Tests use expect/3 to set specific expectations.
